headline: Human Memory
description: Human memory is not a recording device — it's reconstructive and fallible. Types: sensory (milliseconds), short-term/working (seconds, 7±2 items — Miller, 1956), long-term (unlimited capacity). Long-term: explicit (declarative: episodic + semantic) and implicit (procedural skills, conditioning). Consolidation: moving memories from hippocampus to cortex during sleep.

citation.name: The Seven Sins of Memory (Daniel Schacter)
